messages.critical_low: Systolic <80 — shock physiology, rapid evaluation required.
messages.warning_low: Below normal — check perfusion, orthostatic vitals.
messages.critical_high: Hypertensive crisis threshold exceeded — evaluate for end-organ damage.

messages.critical_low: Severe bradycardia — evaluate for heart block, hypothyroid, medication.
messages.warning_low: Bradycardia — check meds (β-blocker, CCB), training state.
messages.warning_high: Tachycardia — investigate cause (pain, fever, dehydration, anemia).
messages.critical_high: Severe tachycardia — rule out arrhythmia, sepsis, hemorrhage, PE.

messages.critical_low: Severe hypoxemia — immediate supplemental O2 and airway assessment.
messages.warning_low: Below normal — ensure proper pulse-ox placement; monitor closely.
Low temperature — recheck, consider environmental vs sepsis (paradoxical hypothermia in elderly).
messages.critical_high: High fever — evaluate for sepsis, consider cultures and aggressive cooling.

messages.critical_low: Severe bradypnea — concern for CNS/opioid effect; support ventilation.
messages.warning_high: Tachypnea — possible infection, metabolic acidosis, PE, anxiety.
messages.critical_high: Severe tachypnea — imminent respiratory failure possible.
Severe pain — prompt assessment and treatment.

messages.critical_low: Severe hypoglycemia — immediate D50 or oral glucose if able.
messages.warning_low: Hypoglycemia — oral glucose, recheck in 15 min.
messages.warning_high: Hyperglycemia — assess symptoms, rule out DKA/HHS.
messages.critical_high: Marked hyperglycemia — check ketones, pH, anion gap.
